raw_input: Write a Python function `is_prime(n: int) -> bool` that returns True iff n is a prime number. Handle n <= 1 correctly (not prime). The function must handle n up to 10_000_000 in under 1 second.

inline.tests:
assert is_prime(2) is True
assert is_prime(3) is True
assert is_prime(4) is False
assert is_prime(1) is False
assert is_prime(0) is False
assert is_prime(-7) is False
assert is_prime(17) is True
assert is_prime(25) is False
assert is_prime(97) is True
assert is_prime(100) is False
assert is_prime(7919) is True
assert is_prime(7920) is False
import time
t0 = time.perf_counter()
assert is_prime(9999991) is True
assert (time.perf_counter() - t0) < 1.0, 'too slow on 10M-range prime'
